pr.title: /ef work (eflib could still use some love)
pr.author: AaronB-Hub

summary: Large effect-system decompilation pass that replaced many /ef stubs with C in efasync.c, efsync.c, and especially eflib.c, while tightening effect, particle, JObj, GObj, and fighter/item type declarations needed by those matches. The PR also exposed an enum-width matching issue: attempts to use FighterKind caused mwcc to treat it as int, so reviewers recommended keeping affected FighterKind-like parameters as s8 for now rather than using the global -enum min option.
what_changed: Implemented a very large efAsync_80063930 switch over effect gfx IDs, replacing stub markers and routing cases through efLib helpers such as efLib_8005C814, efLib_8005C9FC, efLib_8005CAB0, efLib_8005CB34, efLib_8005CD2C, efLib_8005CF40, and hsd_8039EFAC.

Added efAsync static data for effect data-table archives and symbols, including strings such as EfCoData.dat, effCommonDataTable, EfMrData.dat, effMarioDataTable, and a 51-entry efAsync_803C025C table mapping effect data files to data-table names.
Implemented additional efAsync routines: efAsync_8006729C, efAsync_8006730C, efAsync_8006737C, efAsync_8006744C, efAsync_80067624, efAsync_80067688, and efAsync_Spawn.

Implemented many eflib.c functions and callbacks, including lifecycle functions efLib_8005B4B8, efLib_8005B704, efLib_8005B780, efLib_DestroyAll, efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, spawning helpers from efLib_8005C1B4 through efLib_8005D044, and many Effect callback/update functions through efLib_8005FCD8.
Implemented efSync_Spawn as a variadic effect-spawn entry point and updated its header prototype from a loose int-returning declaration to void* efSync_Spawn(s32 gfx_id, HSD_GObj* arg_gobj, ...).
Added ef/inlines.h with GET_EFFECT(gobj), including an M2CTX-specific cast form around HSD_GObjGetUserData.

Refined ef/types.h: Effect now records gobj, parent_gobj, xC as HSD_JObj*, x10 as efLib_UnkCallback taking Effect*, transform as Vec3, and documented size 0x2C; ef_UnkStruct2 and EffectDesc received clearer layouts; ef_UnkStruct3 and _struct_efAsync_803C025C_0xC were introduced.

Refined baselib particle/SRT structures in psappsrt.h: UnkGeneratorMember now has x20_unk and Vec3 x24 instead of separate x24/x28/x2C floats, UnkGeneratorStruct has x4_kind and adjusted fill, and UnkGeneratorStruct2 was added.
Updated baselib particle.h prototypes for several hsd_8039xxxx functions from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void or typed signatures used by the /ef code.
Added offset comments to HSD_Joint fields in jobj.h and annotated possible effect GObj list slots x2C and x30 in gobj.h.
Touched fighter, item, stage, and game-mode callers/types to align with the new effect prototypes and enum-width constraints.
smart_moves: Used centralized efLib helper functions to express repeated effect construction patterns instead of duplicating raw particle/JObj setup in every efAsync switch case.
Preserved matching-sensitive variadic behavior by passing and consuming a va_list-like void* in several helpers, while exposing public spawn APIs as variadic where appropriate.
Added explicit struct offsets and sizes around Effect, EffectDesc, ef_UnkStruct3, HSD_Joint, and particle generator structs, making subsequent /ef and baselib matching work easier to audit.
Introduced GET_EFFECT in ef/inlines.h with an M2CTX branch, keeping decomp-context casts isolated rather than sprinkling conditional casts throughout the effect code.
Kept questionable discoveries as comments instead of overclaiming names, e.g. Effect.x28 is only annotated as possible update control and ef_UnkStruct2 is flagged with uncertainty.
Backed away from replacing s8 fighter-kind parameters with FighterKind enum where it harmed matching, rather than forcing a broader compiler flag that could destabilize many other matches.
decomp_lessons: Do not assume a C enum type will match the original storage width. In this PR, FighterKind as an enum compiled as int, while the asm appeared to require s8 in some call paths.

mwcc has an -enum min option that may make enums use smaller storage, but applying it globally affects all enums and can break many existing matches; local s8 declarations are safer until the project is ready for a global policy change.
For variadic spawn/factory code, helper prototypes may need to accept a va_list-like pointer or void* to preserve call/stack behavior even when the logical API is variadic.
Effect and particle work depends heavily on correct cross-system headers: baselib JObj/GObj/particle prototypes and structs may need to be corrected before /ef functions can match cleanly.

When building large switch-based effect spawn functions, exact constant types and signedness matter: the diff uses explicit U suffixes, s8 parameters, f64 temporaries for M_PI/M_PI_2 decisions, and Vec3 copies before helper calls.
Comments documenting uncertainty are useful in decomp PRs when field purpose is inferred from one function but not yet proven globally.
naming_conventions: Unresolved effect-library symbols kept address-style names such as efLib_8005C814, efLib_8005F990, efAsync_80063930, and efSync_Spawn.
Data-table string symbols use address-style static names such as efAsync_803BFD68 and efAsync_803C025C until better names are known.
Structs with unknown provenance use generated names that include symbol and size context, e.g. _struct_efAsync_803C025C_0xC and _struct_efLib_80458EE0_0x8.
Local variables in newly decompiled code remain descriptive-but-mechanical names such as jobj_1, jobj_2, vec3_1, unkgen_struct_1, unkgen_member_1, ret_obj, f32_1, and f64_1.

Known or semi-known baselib particle names borrow MexTK remarks, e.g. GeneratorAppSRT, ptclGen, and Particle, but the C identifiers remain UnkGeneratorMember, UnkGeneratorStruct, and UnkGeneratorStruct2.
Affected fighter-kind parameters were named ft_kind while remaining typed as s8, with commented-out FighterKind versions left as evidence of the attempted type improvement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x20) in efAsync_80063930 to control stack layout.

Used an #if 1 alternate signature for efAsync_80063930 that takes void* vlist, with the natural variadic version and va_start preserved in the disabled branch, indicating a matching workaround around variadic lowering.
Copied incoming Vec3 arguments to stack locals before calling helper constructors where the asm likely used stack temporaries.
Used f64 temporaries for constants such as M_PI, M_PI_2, and 2 * M_PI before setting JObj rotations, matching common mwcc float/double behavior around math constants.
Used explicit casts through GET_JOBJ, GET_FIGHTER, Effect*, and UnkGeneratorStruct* to guide codegen and field access.
Used exact small integer types and signedness in headers, including s8 for some fighter-kind-like APIs and u8 for HSD_GObj_804D7849.
Replaced generic UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS baselib prototypes with concrete signatures where needed so calls in /ef decompile with correct argument passing and return handling.

review_feedback.author: AaronB-Hub
review_feedback.feedback: Reported remaining errors and suspected FighterKind enum width was causing the issue; asked whether FighterKind could be forced to s8 instead of int.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Said there was no known way to force only FighterKind to s8 and recommended switching affected FighterKind types back to s8 for now.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Noted mwcc -enum min appears to treat FighterKind as s8, but applies to all enums and would likely break many current matches, making it hard to adopt immediately.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Mentioned a similar pattern in lblanguage where enums declared as int were treated by asm as s8 in certain cases, suggesting the same phenomenon might be involved.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename efLib_8005C814, efLib_8005C9FC, efLib_8005CAB0, or efAsync_80063930.
Search for later adoption or rejection of mwcc -enum min in the melee project.
Search for FighterKind parameters that remain typed as s8 due to enum-width matching issues.
Investigate whether Effect.x28 update-control comment was later confirmed or renamed.
Compare later psappsrt.h or particle.h changes for final names of UnkGeneratorMember and UnkGeneratorStruct.
